generations.label: 7th Gen
generations.common_competitors: Acura TLX, Audi A6, BMW 5 Series, Toyota Avalon

known_issues.title: Remote Touch interface lag
known_issues.description: The touchpad-based infotainment interface on pre-2022 models can feel laggy and is a common ergonomics complaint rather than a mechanical fault.
known_issues.symptoms: Cursor input on the center screen responding slowly or overshooting menu selections, most noticeable while driving over bumps.
known_issues.recommendation: Spend time navigating the infotainment menus during the test drive to judge whether the interface bothers you.

known_issues.title: Hybrid battery cooling fan noise
known_issues.description: On ES 300h models, the hybrid battery cooling fan intake beneath the rear seat can get clogged with debris, causing the fan to run louder to compensate.
known_issues.symptoms: A whirring or vacuum-like noise from under the rear seat that increases in hot weather or during hard acceleration.
known_issues.recommendation: On hybrid trims, ask whether the battery cooling filter has been cleaned and listen for fan noise with the rear windows down.

known_issues.title: Panoramic glass roof rattle
known_issues.description: The fixed glass roof trim on higher trims can develop a rattle as the felt-lined seals compress over time.
known_issues.symptoms: A light rattling or ticking noise from the headliner area over rough pavement.
known_issues.recommendation: Drive over rough pavement and listen near the headliner for rattles.

known_issues.title: Front brake rotor warping
known_issues.description: Front rotors on the heavier V6 trim can warp prematurely under hard braking.
known_issues.symptoms: A pulsing sensation through the brake pedal and steering wheel when braking hard from highway speed.
known_issues.recommendation: Brake firmly from highway speed on the test drive and feel for pulsation.

generations.label: 6th Gen
generations.common_competitors: Acura TLX, Infiniti Q50, Toyota Avalon

known_issues.title: Oil cooler O-ring seep
known_issues.description: The 3.5L V6 (2GR-FE) oil-to-water cooler O-ring can harden and seep, allowing a small amount of oil and coolant to mix if left unaddressed.
known_issues.symptoms: A milky discoloration on the oil filler cap or dipstick, or a slow coolant level drop with no visible external leak.
known_issues.recommendation: Pull the oil filler cap and dipstick to check for a milky residue, and ask for recent coolant level history.

known_issues.title: Remote Touch controller sensitivity
known_issues.description: The early mouse-style Remote Touch controller can feel overly sensitive or imprecise, a widely reported usability complaint rather than a failure.
known_issues.symptoms: The on-screen cursor overshooting or jumping past the intended menu item when using the console controller.
known_issues.recommendation: Try operating the navigation and audio menus while stationary to judge the controller for yourself.

known_issues.title: Dashboard trim becoming tacky
known_issues.description: In hot, sun-exposed climates the soft-touch dash coating on this generation can turn sticky as the coating breaks down with UV exposure and heat.
known_issues.symptoms: A tacky or slightly gummy feel to the dashboard and door armrests, sometimes with a faint chemical smell in a hot, closed cabin.
known_issues.recommendation: Touch the dash top and door armrests firmly to check for a sticky residue, especially on cars from hot climates.

known_issues.title: Water pump weeping
known_issues.description: The water pump can begin to weep coolant as it approaches the end of its service life.
known_issues.symptoms: A sweet coolant smell near the front of the engine or a slowly dropping coolant level with an occasional low-coolant warning.
known_issues.recommendation: Check underneath the front of the engine for coolant staining or crust.

generations.label: 5th Gen
generations.common_competitors: Acura TL, Infiniti M, Toyota Avalon

known_issues.title: Dashboard becoming sticky
known_issues.description: This generation is well known in hot climates for a soft-touch dash coating that degrades and turns tacky with prolonged sun and heat exposure.
known_issues.symptoms: A gummy or tacky feel across the dashboard top, door panels, and center console, occasionally with a faint off-gassing smell in a hot cabin.
known_issues.recommendation: Press firmly on the dash top and console in direct sunlight to check for a sticky residue before buying.

known_issues.title: Oil cooler O-ring seep
known_issues.description: The 3.5L V6 (2GR-FE) oil-to-water cooler O-ring can harden with age and allow a small oil or coolant seep.
known_issues.symptoms: A milky film on the dipstick or oil cap, or a gradual coolant loss with no visible puddle underneath.
known_issues.recommendation: Check the dipstick and oil cap for a milky discoloration and ask about recent coolant top-ups.

known_issues.title: Water pump weeping
known_issues.description: The water pump can begin to weep coolant as its seal wears out over time.
known_issues.symptoms: A sweet coolant smell near the front of the engine or a slowly dropping coolant level.
known_issues.recommendation: Inspect underneath the front of the engine for coolant staining.

known_issues.title: Front door speaker rattle
known_issues.description: Factory door speakers can develop a buzz or rattle at higher volumes as the speaker cones age.
known_issues.symptoms: A buzzing or rattling noise from the front doors when the stereo volume is raised, especially with bass-heavy audio.
known_issues.recommendation: Play music at moderate-to-high volume and listen closely near each door.

generations.label: 4th Gen
generations.common_competitors: Acura MDX, Audi Q5, BMW X3, Infiniti QX60

known_issues.title: Remote Touch / touchpad usability complaints
known_issues.description: The center console touchpad interface on this generation is widely reported as fiddly to use while driving compared to a simple touchscreen.
known_issues.symptoms: Difficulty accurately selecting menu items on the center display while the vehicle is moving, sometimes requiring several attempts.
known_issues.recommendation: Spend a few minutes operating the navigation and climate menus during the test drive to see if the controller suits you.

known_issues.title: Oil cooler O-ring seep
known_issues.description: The 3.5L V6 (2GR-FKS) shares a similar oil-to-water cooler design to earlier Toyota/Lexus V6 engines, and the O-ring seal can occasionally seep with age.
known_issues.symptoms: A milky discoloration on the dipstick or filler cap, or a slow, unexplained coolant level drop.
known_issues.recommendation: Check the dipstick for milky residue and review coolant top-up history in service records.

known_issues.title: Hybrid battery cooling fan noise (450h)
known_issues.description: On the RX 450h, the hybrid battery cooling intake beneath the rear seat can collect debris, making the fan run louder than normal.
known_issues.symptoms: A whirring or vacuum-like noise from under the rear seat, most noticeable during hard acceleration or hot weather.
known_issues.recommendation: On hybrid trims, listen for fan noise under the rear seat and ask if the battery cooling filter has been serviced.

known_issues.title: Power liftgate motor slow operation
known_issues.description: The power liftgate motor can slow down or stall partway through its cycle as it ages.
known_issues.symptoms: The power liftgate opening or closing more slowly than normal, or stopping partway and needing a second button press.
known_issues.recommendation: Cycle the power liftgate several times with the key fob to confirm smooth, full-range operation.

maintenance_items.name: AWD transfer case / rear diff fluid

generations.label: 3rd Gen
generations.common_competitors: Acura MDX, Infiniti FX, Mercedes-Benz GLK

known_issues.title: Oil cooler O-ring seep
known_issues.description: The 3.5L V6 (2GR-FE) oil-to-water cooler O-ring can harden with age, allowing a small amount of oil or coolant to seep.
known_issues.symptoms: A milky discoloration on the dipstick or filler cap, or a slow, unexplained coolant loss with no visible external puddle.
known_issues.recommendation: Check the dipstick and filler cap for milky residue and ask about coolant top-up history.

known_issues.title: Dashboard trim becoming tacky
known_issues.description: Cars from hot, sun-heavy climates can develop a sticky soft-touch dash coating as the material breaks down with UV and heat exposure over time.
known_issues.symptoms: A gummy or tacky feel to the dashboard top and door armrests, sometimes with a faint chemical odor in a closed, hot cabin.
known_issues.recommendation: Press on the dash top and armrests in direct sunlight to check for stickiness before buying.

known_issues.title: Water pump weeping
known_issues.description: The water pump seal can start to weep coolant as it nears the end of its service life.
known_issues.symptoms: A sweet coolant smell or coolant staining near the front of the engine, occasionally paired with a low coolant warning.
known_issues.recommendation: Inspect underneath the front of the engine for coolant residue or crust.

known_issues.title: Remote Touch controller precision complaints
known_issues.description: The mouse-style Remote Touch console controller is a commonly cited ergonomics annoyance rather than a mechanical defect on this generation.
known_issues.symptoms: The on-screen cursor jumping past the intended selection or feeling imprecise when operating the infotainment menus.
known_issues.recommendation: Operate the navigation and audio menus while parked to judge whether the controller works for you.

maintenance_items.name: AWD transfer case / rear diff fluid

generations.label: 2nd Gen
generations.common_competitors: Acura MDX, Infiniti FX35, Mercedes-Benz M-Class

known_issues.title: Dashboard becoming sticky
known_issues.description: This generation is one of the most commonly cited for a dashboard coating that turns tacky and gummy in hot, sun-exposed climates, occasionally prompting owner class-action claims.
known_issues.symptoms: A sticky or gummy feel across the dash top, door panels, and console, with a noticeable texture change compared to a cool-climate car.
known_issues.recommendation: Press firmly on the dash and door trim in direct sunlight; a sticky feel is common and should factor into your offer.

known_issues.title: ABS actuator issue (RX 400h hybrid)
known_issues.description: Early RX 400h hybrid models were subject to a documented brake actuator software issue that could cause reduced regenerative braking assist under certain conditions, addressed by a factory recall.
known_issues.symptoms: A firmer-than-normal brake pedal feel or a warning light related to the brake or hybrid system, most relevant on unrepaired hybrid examples.
known_issues.recommendation: On 400h models, confirm the brake actuator recall was completed using the VIN on the manufacturer's recall lookup.

known_issues.title: Water pump and timing belt service
known_issues.description: The V6 engines in this generation use a timing belt design where the water pump is driven off the same belt, both needing replacement together at a set interval.
known_issues.symptoms: No early warning symptoms in most cases; a neglected belt risks a sudden water pump failure or, in rare cases, engine damage if it fails.
known_issues.recommendation: Confirm from service records whether the timing belt and water pump have been replaced on schedule.

known_issues.title: Front suspension bushing wear
known_issues.description: Front lower control arm bushings wear out over time, causing a looser, less precise steering feel.
known_issues.symptoms: A slight clunk over bumps and a vaguer, less direct steering feel at highway speed.
known_issues.recommendation: Listen for clunks over bumps and note steering feel during the test drive.

generations.label: 2nd Gen (Facelift)
generations.common_competitors: Land Rover Discovery, Mercedes-Benz GLE, Toyota 4Runner

known_issues.title: Air suspension (AHC) compressor wear
known_issues.description: Vehicles equipped with the optional Adaptive Variable Suspension / height control air compressor can develop a failing compressor or leaking air spring over time, though the standard steel-spring GX is unaffected.
known_issues.symptoms: The vehicle sitting noticeably lower on one corner, a warning message about the suspension system, or a labored pumping noise from under the rear when starting the vehicle.
known_issues.recommendation: Check ride height is level on all four corners after the vehicle has sat overnight, and ask if it has the air suspension option.

known_issues.title: Remote Touch controller ergonomics
known_issues.description: The console-mounted Remote Touch controller used on this generation is commonly described as fiddly to operate accurately while driving.
known_issues.symptoms: Difficulty precisely selecting menu items on the center display while the vehicle is in motion.
known_issues.recommendation: Try operating the infotainment controller during the test drive to see if it suits your preference.

known_issues.title: Third-row seat storage complexity
known_issues.description: The side-hinged rear cargo door and manually stowed third row are a common ergonomic complaint but not a mechanical defect.
known_issues.symptoms: A heavy, awkward third-row seat that must be manually folded and does not fold flat, reducing usable cargo space compared to rivals.
known_issues.recommendation: Practice folding the third row and loading cargo through the side-hinged door before buying if you need frequent cargo flexibility.

known_issues.title: Fuel economy shortfall vs. competitors
known_issues.description: The 4.6L V8 delivers notably worse fuel economy than most modern midsize luxury SUV rivals, a widely known tradeoff rather than a fault.
known_issues.symptoms: Real-world fuel economy noticeably below the window sticker rating, especially in city driving or with a roof rack installed.
known_issues.recommendation: Budget for higher fuel costs than typical midsize SUVs when comparing total ownership cost.

generations.label: 2nd Gen (Pre-Facelift)
generations.common_competitors: Land Rover LR4, Mercedes-Benz GL, Toyota 4Runner

known_issues.title: Air suspension (AHC) compressor wear
known_issues.description: On vehicles with the height control air suspension option, the compressor or air springs can wear out and begin leaking over time.
known_issues.symptoms: The vehicle sitting lower on one or more corners, a suspension warning message, or an audible compressor running longer than normal after startup.
known_issues.recommendation: Check that ride height is even on all four corners after the vehicle sits overnight.

known_issues.title: Early production VSC software recalibration
known_issues.description: The first model year of this generation received a factory software update to the Vehicle Stability Control system to improve handling response in aggressive maneuvers; unrepaired examples are now rare but worth confirming.
known_issues.symptoms: No noticeable symptom in daily driving; the concern was specific to abrupt handling behavior during hard evasive maneuvers on unrepaired vehicles.
known_issues.recommendation: Check the VIN against the manufacturer recall database to confirm the stability control update was completed.

known_issues.title: Remote Touch controller ergonomics
known_issues.description: The console-mounted mouse-style controller is a commonly cited usability complaint rather than a reliability issue on this generation.
known_issues.symptoms: Imprecise or slow cursor movement when navigating infotainment menus while driving.
known_issues.recommendation: Operate the console controller during the test drive to judge usability for yourself.

known_issues.title: Timing belt service interval
known_issues.description: The 4.6L V8 uses a timing belt (not a chain), which must be replaced at a set interval to avoid a costly failure.
known_issues.symptoms: No early warning in most cases; a belt that is not replaced on schedule risks sudden failure and potential engine damage.
known_issues.recommendation: Verify from service records whether the timing belt has been replaced on schedule.

generations.label: 1st Gen
generations.common_competitors: Land Rover Discovery, Mercedes-Benz M-Class, Acura MDX

known_issues.title: Air suspension (AHC) compressor and air spring wear
known_issues.description: The height-adjustable air suspension fitted to most GX 470s is a well-documented wear item as the vehicle ages, with compressors and air springs eventually needing replacement.
known_issues.symptoms: The rear or front of the vehicle sagging lower than normal, an audible compressor cycling frequently, or a suspension warning light on the dash.
known_issues.recommendation: Check ride height on all corners after the vehicle has been parked overnight, and budget for eventual air suspension repair or conversion to coil springs.

known_issues.title: Dashboard trim becoming tacky
known_issues.description: Vehicles from hot, sun-heavy climates can develop a sticky, degraded dash coating as the soft-touch material breaks down with age and UV exposure.
known_issues.symptoms: A gummy or tacky feel to the dash top and door trim, most pronounced on vehicles that spent years outdoors in hot climates.
known_issues.recommendation: Press on the dash top and door trim in direct sunlight to check for a sticky residue.

known_issues.title: Timing belt and water pump service
known_issues.description: The 4.7L V8 uses a timing belt with the water pump driven off the same belt, both requiring replacement together at a set interval.
known_issues.symptoms: No early warning signs in most cases; deferred replacement risks sudden belt or water pump failure.
known_issues.recommendation: Confirm from service records whether the timing belt and water pump have been replaced on schedule.

known_issues.title: Front lower control arm bushing wear
known_issues.description: Front suspension bushings wear out over time and years of off-road-capable use, loosening steering feel.
known_issues.symptoms: A vaguer, looser steering feel at highway speed and occasional clunking over bumps from the front suspension.
known_issues.recommendation: Note steering precision and listen for clunks over bumps during the test drive.

generations.label: 3rd Gen
generations.common_competitors: Acura TLX, Audi A4, BMW 3 Series, Infiniti Q50

known_issues.title: Oil cooler O-ring seep
known_issues.description: The 3.5L V6 (2GR-FSE) direct-injection engine's oil-to-water cooler O-ring can harden with age, occasionally allowing a small oil or coolant seep.
known_issues.symptoms: A milky discoloration on the dipstick or oil filler cap, or a slow, unexplained coolant level drop.
known_issues.recommendation: Check the dipstick and filler cap for milky residue and review coolant top-up history.

known_issues.title: Run-flat tire ride and wear complaints
known_issues.description: F Sport and higher trims are fitted with run-flat tires, which are known for a firmer ride and faster, more expensive wear than standard tires.
known_issues.symptoms: A noticeably stiffer ride over rough pavement and tread wearing out faster than typical, with no spare tire included.
known_issues.recommendation: Check remaining tread depth and ask if the car has ever needed a run-flat tire replaced, which can be costly.

known_issues.title: Remote Touch interface lag
known_issues.description: The touchpad-based infotainment controller on pre-2017 models can feel imprecise and laggy, a widely reported ergonomics complaint.
known_issues.symptoms: The on-screen cursor overshooting menu selections or responding slowly to touchpad input while driving.
known_issues.recommendation: Try the touchpad controller during the test drive to judge whether it suits your preference.

known_issues.title: Front brake rotor and pad wear
known_issues.description: The sport-tuned brakes on F Sport trims wear faster than standard trims, especially with spirited driving.
known_issues.symptoms: Increased brake dust, a grinding or squealing noise when pads are low, or a pulsing sensation from warped rotors under hard braking.
known_issues.recommendation: Ask about brake pad and rotor replacement history, particularly on F Sport trims.

generations.label: 2nd Gen
generations.common_competitors: Acura TSX, Audi A4, BMW 3 Series, Infiniti G37

known_issues.title: Oil cooler O-ring seep (IS 350 / IS F)
known_issues.description: The V6 and V8 direct-injection engines on the higher trims can develop a hardened oil-to-water cooler O-ring that seeps oil or coolant over time.
known_issues.symptoms: A milky discoloration on the dipstick or filler cap, or a slow coolant level drop with no visible external leak.
known_issues.recommendation: Check the dipstick and filler cap for milky residue and ask about coolant service history.

known_issues.title: Dashboard trim becoming tacky
known_issues.description: Cars from hot, sun-exposed climates can develop a sticky, degraded soft-touch dash coating as the material breaks down over time.
known_issues.symptoms: A gummy or tacky feel to the dashboard top and door armrests, most common on vehicles that spent years in hot climates.
known_issues.recommendation: Press on the dash top and armrests in direct sunlight to check for stickiness.

known_issues.title: Water pump weeping
known_issues.description: The water pump seal can begin to weep coolant as it nears the end of its service life.
known_issues.symptoms: A sweet coolant smell or staining near the front of the engine, occasionally with a low coolant warning.
known_issues.recommendation: Inspect underneath the front of the engine for coolant residue.

known_issues.title: Rear seat legroom / trunk space limitations
known_issues.description: The tight rear seat and shallow trunk are a common ownership complaint on this generation rather than a mechanical defect.
known_issues.symptoms: Limited rear legroom for taller passengers and a smaller trunk opening than most competitors of the era.
known_issues.recommendation: Sit in the rear seat and load the trunk yourself before buying if space is a priority.

generations.label: 1st Gen
generations.common_competitors: Acura TL, Audi A4, BMW 3 Series

known_issues.title: Dashboard becoming sticky
known_issues.description: This generation is commonly cited in hot climates for a soft-touch dash coating that degrades and turns tacky with prolonged sun and heat exposure.
known_issues.symptoms: A gummy or tacky feel across the dash top and door trim, with a visibly different texture than a cool-climate example.
known_issues.recommendation: Press on the dash and door trim in direct sunlight to check for a sticky residue before buying.

known_issues.title: Automatic transmission valve body wear
known_issues.description: High-mileage examples can develop rough or delayed shifts as the transmission valve body wears.
known_issues.symptoms: A delayed or harsh shift between gears, particularly noticeable from a stop or during light acceleration.
known_issues.recommendation: Test drive through varied speeds and note any harsh or delayed shifts.

known_issues.title: Rear differential seal seep (manual trims)
known_issues.description: The limited-slip rear differential fitted to some manual-transmission cars can develop a minor seal seep over time.
known_issues.symptoms: A small oil spot or light staining underneath the rear of the vehicle near the differential housing.
known_issues.recommendation: Check underneath the rear differential for oil staining, especially on manual-transmission cars.

known_issues.title: Interior trim rattles
known_issues.description: Interior trim panels and the console can develop rattles as the plastics and clips age.
known_issues.symptoms: Buzzing or rattling noises from the dash, console, or door panels over rough pavement.
known_issues.recommendation: Drive over a rough road section and listen for interior rattles.
